raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased some clothing from XXXX ( a XXXX clothing company it turns out ), and some of the items were terrible quality ( see through, material that catches easily, mismatched seams, etc ). I notified them and asked for a return shipping label and they said they told me I could keep the items for free and get store credit or they would offer me a 50 % refund in cash and I could keep the items. I told them there were quality concerns and they are supposed to send me a return label per THEIR website. Then they told me that they didn't consider see through material, fabric that catches easily and mismatched seams " quality issues '', and that if I wanted a refund I would have to mail it back ( to XXXX ) myself. This is unacceptable. I purchased these items believing I could easily return them if there were issues and that has definitely not been the case. 

I raised a dispute with American Express explaining this and they said that since XXXX provided an address for me to mail the returns to, I am not entitled to a refund - but the issue is not the mailing address, it's that XXXX should be providing ME with a prepaid return label because the issues I raised were valid and XXXX can't just decide what issues they consider to be quality issues or not when it's convenient to them. There website clearly states that if there are quality issues, they will provide prepaid return labels to customers. But in practice they do NOT do that. I'm not paying {$40.00} to ship their defective clothes back to them.
